rationale: Contract/no-regression lens satisfied. The gate.go and tally.go diffs are PURELY ADDITIVE (zero deletion lines): every pre-existing leg (1-10 in PanelGateDecision; the REJECT/threshold legs in VoteDecision) is byte-preserved. Leg 9.5 sits correctly between leg 9 (REJECT, gate.go:248) and leg 10 (threshold, :283); the VoteDecision twin sits between its REJECT (:225) and threshold-pass (:237), so the vote-only twin cannot diverge from the gate on the vote portion. UnresolvedVerdicts() iterates the already-slot-sorted latest-round Verdicts and excludes REJECT (deliberately leg 9's business), so ordering is deterministic. Scanned every assertion-changing test line: the ONLY expected-outcome flips are the four named ones (panel_decision 5A+1->Block; advisory rename->Dissent_WouldBlock; cmd at-threshold->wantErr; votedecision 5/6->VoteBlock); all other test edits are input-only (RC/empty fillers -> genuine APPROVEs, or a recorded approve_threshold to keep the boundary Pass) with unchanged outcomes. panel/complete/cmd all green in isolation. Scope: 9 files, no refutation/AppliedRefutations code (Bead 2's), no internal/bead, git clean.
findings: NO REGRESSION: gate.go/tally.go diff has zero deletion lines (verified via git diff | grep '^-'); all pre-existing legs byte-preserved, leg 9.5 and the VoteDecision twin are strictly additive and correctly ordered (between REJECT and threshold).

4 intended flips confirmed and exhaustive: (a) panel_decision_test.go '5 APPROVE + 1 dissent' want Allow->Block; (b) panel_advisory_test.go TestPanelAdvisory_Passing_WouldPass renamed to _Dissent_WouldBlock (asserts 'would BLOCK') + NEW all-APPROVE _Passing_WouldPass; (c) cmd/mindspec panel_test.go 'passing (at-threshold)' split into a wantErr:true dissent row + all-APPROVE exit-0 companion; (d) votedecision_test.go '5/6 -> Pass' -> VoteBlock + new 6/6 all-APPROVE Pass. No unclassified outcome flip anywhere.

8 outcome-preserving input updates confirmed: panel_decision 3/3 (RC->3rd APPROVE, stays Allow); panel_test.go x3 staleness/gate-inertness fixtures (5A+1RC -> 6/6 all-APPROVE, stays Allow); panel_gate_e2e approveVerdicts helper (N-1+RC -> all-APPROVE for staleness/dirty/fail-open subjects); cmd panel_test.go second 'at-threshold' row (all-APPROVE + ApproveThresholdExpr '6', stays Allow); instruct panelstate beadPanel helper fills real APPROVE/REJECT/RC and at_threshold_fresh/RenderPanelState_Pass restructured to 6/6 + recorded threshold 6, GatePass preserved. Every one keeps its prior outcome.

z4ps flake CONFIRMED pre-existing and unrelated: instruct TestRun_IdleNoBeads fails because it reads the LIVE repo state and finds spec-114 in 'implement' mode (we run inside the spec-114 worktree), rendering the implement template instead of 'No Active Work'. It lives in run_test.go which this bead did not touch; the only instruct change (panelstate_test.go beadPanel helper) has no os.Chdir/Setenv/global/TestMain and cannot reach it. Would fail identically on base ccdc3296. NOT a regression.

Cross-package combined `go test` run also showed transient cmd/mindspec + instruct failures that vanish on isolated -count=1 runs (all three packages green alone) — the known 7y4d shared-cwd isolation class, pre-existing, not introduced here.

Empirical: go build ./... exit 0; gofmt -l ./cmd ./internal empty; go vet ./internal/panel ./internal/complete clean; go test -count=1 ./internal/panel, ./internal/complete, ./cmd/mindspec all ok in isolation.
Scope fence held: exactly 9 files, no internal/bead, no Refut/AppliedRefutations symbols in production (grep NONE) — refutation correctly deferred to Bead 2. git status clean after review.

Non-blocking note (no action for Bead 1): with leg 9.5 present, leg 10's Block branch becomes effectively unreachable in normal flow (a complete, REJECT-free, unresolved-free panel is necessarily all-APPROVE and therefore >= N-1 threshold). This is the intended layering, the code is preserved verbatim, and TestPanelGateDecision_ThresholdFloorIsLayeredNotReplaced proves the floor still Blocks when exercised directly. No regression.
